steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has an independent clause followed by 'though,' a subordinating conjunction that introduces a dependent clause. The question asks which punctuation (if any) correctly joins them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma before 'though': a comma separates 'though' from the clause it introduces — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — colon before 'though': a colon cannot precede a subordinating conjunction mid-sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — semicolon before 'though': a semicolon requires two independent clauses; the 'though' clause is dependent. ✗
  • Choice D — no punctuation: the dependent clause introduced by 'though' attaches cleanly to the independent clause without any punctuation. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
